notes: Token & cost extraction for a single turn is covered by basic-turn; this scenario stresses per-turn accumulation across MULTIPLE turns. OpenCode runs each turn headlessly via a blocking `opencode run` (the driver chains --session for continuity); session state lives in OpenCode's SQLite store, observed by the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source as the UUID-keyed ses_… session. The phase DSL only asserts state arcs and event kinds (no TotalTokens / cache-bucket metric form), so the token-accounting evidence lives in events.jsonl: each step-finish emits a PerTurnContribution whose UsageBreakdown carries input/output and a nested cache object (cache.read → CacheRead, cache.write → CacheCreation5m), and SessionMetrics.TotalTokens accumulates monotonically. After turn 1 TotalTokens > 0; after turn 2 and turn 3 TotalTokens ≥ the previous total. The recording captures three turns so the monotonic-accumulation invariant is visible across two boundaries (T1→T2, T2→T3). session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; t1_start (matching working) establishes the session identity that later phases chain off via same_session_as. The `no state_transition to waiting` invariant covers tool_calls_max:0 — three no-tool turns never gate on a permission ask.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch; never enters waiting (no tool use)
PID bound to the ses_… session
Turn 1 user prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
Turn 1 completes back to ready on the same ses_… session; first PerTurnContribution flushed, TotalTokens > 0
Turn 2 user prompt → working on the same ses_… session
Turn 2 completes back to ready; TotalTokens after T2 ≥ TotalTokens after T1 (monotonic)
Turn 3 user prompt → working on the same ses_… session
Turn 3 completes back to ready; TotalTokens after T3 ≥ TotalTokens after T2 (monotonic accumulation across 3 turns)
